title: Is air conditioning good or bad? Join this (fake) group chat for (real) analysis
subhead: Each time a new heatwave hits, the question of air conditioning and its environmental impacts crops up in the public debate. Here are the key points to help you understand what is at stake.

'Behind the suffering there are people who love life, art and peace': Words of hope from a poetry contest in Gaza
subhead: About 60 students from Gaza, aged 18 to 25, responded to a call from the French Institute of Gaza and the NGO Academic Solidarity with Palestine by submitting poems or videos in which they shared, in French, their feelings and hopes in the face of the war that has affected them for almost three years.

title: Spain set up World Cup semi-final with France after late win against Belgium
subhead: Spain beat Belgium 2-1, paving the way for a World Cup semi-final against France in Arlington, Texas this coming Tuesday.

title: 'Foreign Tongues': The Rolling Stones ride a second wave of youth with their new album
subhead: Without reinventing their formula, Mick Jagger and Keith Richards have delivered a catchy and revitalizing record that makes listeners forget the more uneven albums of the past few decades.

title: Marine Le Pen conviction: 'It's very French to think that preventing one person from running undermines democracy as a whole'
subhead: In the far-right leader's appeals trial, judges once again confirmed the courts' wariness of intervening too heavily in the political arena.

subhead: Barred from entering or performing due to antisemitic and neo-Nazi statements, the American rapper is set to take the stage on July 11 in Tirana. Many protesters are accusing Prime Minister Edi Rama of using the event to serve his own interests.

title: French AI defense chief: 'Our challenge is to confront the malicious uses of AI without betraying our values'
subhead: Innovation in military applications of AI technology is part of a power struggle in which US companies try to prevent the emergence of international competitors, says Bertrand Rondepierre, director of the ministerial agency for defense AI.
